title: WF-015 hardening — suspicious-login scan (brute-force detection -> alert -> incident)
business_reason: The security.suspicious_login_scan job stub existed but did nothing. Detect brute-force / credential-stuffing — repeated failed logins for one identity — raise a security_alert, and open an incident at high risk. Reuses the same scan->alert->incident + shared-runner + sweep pattern as drift/audit.

design.scan: server/security/suspiciousLogins.ts: PURE scanSuspiciousLogins(events, threshold=5) groups login_failed events by identity (email -> IP -> staff) and flags any at/over the threshold; severity escalates (>=5 high, >=10 critical), risk_score = min(100, failures*10). maxSeverity() drives incident severity. Unit-tested (6).
design.runner: server/security/suspiciousLoginsRun.ts: runSuspiciousLoginScan(supabase, actor) loads recent failed logins, runs the scan, UPSERTs security_alerts (idempotent alert_code per identity; alert_source login_monitor, alert_type brute_force), and opens (or reuses) an unauthorized_access incident on high/critical; audited. Shared by the route + the sweep job.
design.sweep: implemented the security.suspicious_login_scan job handler and added it to the daily /api/cron/security-sweep (now 3 jobs).
design.ui: a 'Suspicious logins' panel on the Audit Integrity & Drift console.

param_14.question: How do we catch brute-force or credential-stuffing attempts?
param_14.answer: A scan groups recent failed logins by identity (email, then IP, then staff). Any identity over the failed-attempt threshold raises a brute_force security alert (severity scaling with the count), and a high/critical one opens an unauthorized_access incident. It runs on-demand from the console and automatically in the daily security sweep; alerts are idempotent.
param_14.answer_source: staff_login_events (login_failed), security_alerts, the security sweep, WF-015 hardening
param_14.security_implications: detects brute-force / credential-stuffing; severity + risk_score scale with attempt volume; high/critical -> incident; idempotent alerts (no duplicate noise); runs unattended via the sweep.
param_14.approval_implications: none (detection); the alert lifecycle (new -> acknowledged -> escalated/converted_to_incident -> resolved) follows.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: impossible-travel / new-device signals; auto-lock after N failures; per-IP rate-based alerting; notify on alert.
